content.question: Would abolishing the 6/8-year track (consolidating to a single entry age) be justifiable under Hungary's Fundamental Law as a reasonable regulation of the modality of choice, or would it face Constitutional Court challenge as an impermissible restriction on the constitutionally protected right to free choice of school?
content.reduction_path: Commission a formal constitutional-law opinion from established Hungarian constitutional scholars or request an advisory opinion from the Constitutional Court. Separately, conduct a comparative-law review of how other EU member states with early-tracking systems have legislated changes to entry age (e.g., Germany's Länder reforms) and whether any such changes have been tested in court; use those precedents to refine the constitutional argument for or against the restrictiveness of reform.
